description: Back at the Lighthouse, Deke tries to connect with Daisy, confronting his feelings for her but learning she remains emotionally distant and still mourns Lincoln. Daisy reveals her vulnerabilities and Deke admits his relation to Fitz and Simmons, to Daisy's surprise. Their conversation strengthens their bond before the next phase of their mission.

transcript.line: What's in the bag?
My mum.
Oh. I heard about your little dust-up. I just wanted to make sure you were all right.
I've been better. It's like the universe keeps reminding me that I should never have come back from the future. Also, some creep put a bunch of lemons on my bed as some weird sort of prank.

Sounds like classic Fitz. Look, I, um, I feel like you're exactly where you're supposed to be. I, on the other hand, I... I lived in the Lighthouse my entire life, only to time-travel into the past and still spend every single day of my life inside the freaking Lighthouse. So it's like, even without Kasius gone, this place just has this leash that keeps pulling me back in.
Well, I'm glad you've stuck around. Even if it's a little crazy.
Maybe it's not that crazy. I've been wanting to tell you something, since we've gotten... you know, I don't wanna say closer but...
You really don't. Everyone who gets close to me ends up dying.
Me too. And that's why I feel like we have so much in common.
My mum...
No, I know, and...
Lincoln.
Who's Lincoln?

Uh, Lincoln Campbell. He... He was the first Inhuman that I became close with. Fought at our side and he... he died. He died for me, really. Right when we were getting going, or, you know, getting good.
Well, sounds like you were really in love with this guy.
Kind of still am. Which is why I... I can't lose someone else. And Coulson means more to me than anyone. And if he goes...
No, no, no. We'll... We're gonna find a way to save him.
Sorry, you... You were gonna tell me something?
Yeah, Fitz-Simmons are my grandparents.
What? Wait, what?
I know.
Are you serious?
Mm-hm.
Do they know?
Yeah. Yeah. Cat's totally out of the food sack.
Oh, my God. That makes so much sense. You and Fitz are both so... You... You're such special people.
Good. I... I was worried that it was gonna make things weird between us.
Wait, why would it make things weird?
No reason.
This man is clearly a charlatan. No mortal being can wield the power of gravitonium. Especially not some human. I submit we strip him to rags, then put him to auction.
That would be most unwise, Estella. He has somehow harnessed gravitonium within himself.
Even so, it has no bearing. This man is not of the Confederacy. He has no business here.
Well then maybe it's time you made me a part of your little secret society.
Impossible! For centuries the Confederacy has had but six members. To add another would desecrate the order.
This is like a room full of Democrats. You get your rules and your regulations. Who's really in charge here?
This is a Confederacy. All voices are equal.
Though Crixon is often considered the most wise amongst us.
The fact remains there is simply no place for a human amongst us.
Your mistake is thinking I'm still human.
Well, it looks like there's a spot for me here after all.
